text.title: Pattern Memory Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Pattern Memory application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Capture MUST require ≥2 distinct contexts where solution worked; single-context wins rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-context wins are coincidences, not patterns.

rule.statement: Confidence MUST start at 0.5 and graduate by +0.1 per successful use; arbitrary scores rejected.
rule.rationale: Arbitrary scores undermine the confidence signal.

rule.statement: Patterns covered by existing linter rules MUST NOT be captured.
rule.rationale: Capturing the obvious dilutes the signal.

rule.statement: Patterns with confidence ≥0.8 MUST sync to CLAUDE.md.
rule.rationale: Un-synced patterns at high confidence waste the win.

rule.statement: Every pattern MUST cite ≥1 rationale (why it works, not just what it is).
rule.rationale: Rationale-less patterns get applied where they don't fit.
